info.title: Woes REST API
info.summary: Public REST API for workspace conversations, issues, clients, and agents.
info.description: Use workspace API keys to work with Woes support conversations, messages, issues, client account aggregates, and public agent metadata from server-side integrations.

tags.name: Conversations
tags.description: Create, read, update, and message support conversations.

tags.name: Issues
tags.description: Create, read, and update support issues.

tags.name: Clients
tags.description: Read client account aggregates derived from workspace conversations.

tags.name: Agents
tags.description: Read named support agent metadata and public widget keys.

get.description: Lists recent workspace conversations visible to the API key workspace.

post.description: Creates an API-origin conversation in the authenticated workspace.

get.description: Retrieves one workspace-scoped conversation. Cross-workspace ids return 404.

patch.description: Updates supported public fields on a workspace-scoped conversation.

get.description: Lists ordered messages for a workspace-scoped conversation.

post.description: Appends a user or assistant message to a workspace-scoped conversation.

value.body: I am getting a 401 from POST /orders.

example.body: I am getting a 401 from POST /orders.

get.description: Lists recent workspace issues visible to the API key workspace.

value.subject: OAuth redirect URI mismatch
value.description: Customer reports a redirect_uri mismatch on the production OAuth app.

example.subject: OAuth redirect URI mismatch
example.description: Customer reports a redirect_uri mismatch on the production OAuth app.

get.description: Retrieves one workspace-scoped issue. Cross-workspace ids return 404.

patch.description: Updates supported public fields on a workspace-scoped issue.

get.description: Lists client account aggregates derived from conversations in the API key workspace.
Retrieves one client account aggregate from the API key workspace. Missing or cross-workspace ids return 404.
Lists public metadata for named support agents in the API key workspace. Prompt/config internals and learned profiles are not returned.
Retrieves one public agent metadata object from the API key workspace. Missing or cross-workspace ids return 404.

workspaceApiKey.description: Workspace API key that starts with woesk_. Paste your own key into the docs playground; never expose it in browser code.

ClientId.name: clientId
ClientId.description: Client id returned by the Clients API.

ClientHealth.name: health
ClientHealth.description: Filter clients by computed health label.

ClientSearch.name: search
ClientSearch.description: Search by client id, name, channel, or contact.

AgentId.name: agentId
AgentId.description: Agent id returned by the Agents API.

AgentSearch.name: search
AgentSearch.description: Search by agent id, name, or public widget key.

AgentIsDefault.name: isDefault
AgentIsDefault.description: Filter to default or non-default agents.

Unauthorized.description: Missing, unknown, revoked, or expired API key.

value.error: A valid workspace API key is required.

InsufficientScope.description: API key does not include the required scope.

value.error: This API key is missing the required scope.

NotFound.description: Record not found in the API key workspace.

Conflict.description: Record conflicts with an existing workspace record.

value.error: A record with this identifier already exists.
Too many API requests. Slow down and try again shortly.
The API request could not be completed.

example.body: I am getting a 401 from POST /orders.

example.subject: OAuth redirect URI mismatch
example.description: Customer reports a redirect_uri mismatch on the production OAuth app.

widgetPublicKey.description: Public widget key for this agent. This is not a REST API credential.

attachedSourceCount.description: Number of API context sources attached to the agent.

Agent.description: Public agent metadata. Prompt/config internals, learned profiles, attached source ids, provider details, and operator-only debug data are intentionally omitted.
